summary: Memory bandwidth bottleneck with 70% DRAM utilization and severe long scoreboard stalls from global memory latency
reasoning: The kernel achieves 70.38% DRAM throughput and 92.12% compute-memory throughput, indicating memory-bound behavior. The dominant stall reason is long scoreboard at 57.37%, which indicates warps are waiting for global memory operations to complete. Despite high L1 hit rate (88.62%), the inefficient memory access pattern with only 46.71% global load efficiency wastes bandwidth.

root_causes.cause: Poor global memory load efficiency causing wasted bandwidth

evidence.interpretation: Less than half of loaded bytes are actually used, indicating uncoalesced or misaligned memory accesses
Reading ~1GB but only ~47% is useful data, wasting significant memory bandwidth

root_causes.cause: High memory latency stalls blocking warp execution

evidence.interpretation: Warps spend 57% of active time waiting for global memory operations, indicating insufficient latency hiding
DRAM throughput at 70% of peak suggests memory subsystem is heavily utilized but not fully saturated due to access patterns

recommended_fixes.fix: Restructure memory access to load contiguous BLOCK_W elements per warp using vectorized loads (tl.load with proper alignment) and ensure each thread loads consecutive addresses to achieve coalesced 128-byte transactions
recommended_fixes.rationale: Improving global load efficiency from 46.71% to near 100% would nearly double effective memory bandwidth, reducing both total memory traffic and long scoreboard stalls by allowing more data per memory transaction
